message:
Add dynamic viewport height calculation and terminal height clamping to prevent alternate-screen scrolling

- interactive.rs: Add view_effective_conversation_height() that computes
  the actual available viewport per render frame, accounting for
  conditional chrome (scroll indicator, tool status bar, status message,
  input area vs processing spinner). Replace static
  conversation_viewport.height with effective height in view() for line
  slicing and scroll indicator logic.
- interactive.rs: Add clamp_to_terminal_height() that ensures view output
  never exceeds term_height rows (at most term_height-1 newlines),
  preventing the cursor from advancing past the last visible row in the
  alternate-screen buffer.
- tui_state.rs: Add tui_state_tool_update_with_diff_details_appends_diff_block
  test verifying that ToolUpdate with a details.diff JSON field appends
  a formatted diff block to the conversation output.

Previously, conditional UI elements (tool spinner, status message) could
cause the total output to exceed terminal height, triggering unwanted
scrolling in alternate-screen mode. The new per-frame calculation and
final clamp eliminate this off-by-one class of bugs entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
